findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly enters rooms, cars, and people through the same smell-led sentence shape. After the third or fourth instance, the material detail stops feeling chosen and starts feeling like the default descriptive engine, which flattens texture variance across an otherwise well-observed chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the descriptive entries at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:177, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:335 so no more than one of those beats relies on a `smelled of` construction. Keep the class and labor specificity, but redistribute the sensory work toward touch, sound, motion, or spatial pressure in the revised lines.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, when rereading snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:139, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:177, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:335, at least three of those entries no longer use a `smelled of` formula and instead lead with a different concrete sensory channel while preserving the same material specificity. Fail if multiple scene or character introductions still arrive through the same smell-led construction.

findings.problem: A negation-first correction tic recurs often enough to become visible: the prose tells the reader what a feeling or object is not before naming what it is. Used once, that can sharpen judgment; repeated across the chapter, it gives the narration a self-correcting, over-authored cadence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the sentence pairs at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:249, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:407 so at least two of the three moments state the concrete judgment directly on first pass instead of using a `not this, but that` turn. Keep the meaning, but lead with the actual comparison or recognition rather than the rhetorical correction.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in at least two of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:249, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:407, the revised text no longer uses an `It was not ... It was ...` or equivalent negative-correction pair and still lands the same meaning through direct concrete phrasing. Fail if the same self-correcting sentence shape remains in all three places.

summary: The chapter cleanly satisfies the ending brief: Beni's downgrade into airport-night work is concrete, Eszter grants honesty and limited contact without romantic closure, continuity holds, and the final decision to stay on the main road lands as an earned revision of the novel's secrecy pattern. Dialogue voices stay distinct and contraction-natural. The fail is sentence-level prose control: repeated smell-led scene entries and repeated negation-first corrections become noticeable tics and flatten the chapter's texture enough to keep this lens below pass level.
